data.title: OSI Systems (NASDAQ: OSIS) to report EPS of $2.52 on 2026-01-29. Historical EPS beats, share price increase by 14.21%. Stock trading at $276.97, up 39.28% over last 52 weeks. Analysts rate as Buy, with target price at $287.33.
data.description: OSI Systems will release its quarterly earnings on January 29th, with analysts predicting an EPS of $2.52. Investors hope to see results surpassing estimates and positive guidance for the next quarter. In the last quarter, OSI beat EPS by $0.05, leading to a 14.21% increase in share price the next day. In the past quarters, OSI has generally outperformed EPS estimates, resulting in positive price changes. As of January 27, shares were trading at $276.97, with a 39.28% increase over the last 52 weeks. Analysts have given OSI Systems a consensus Buy rating, with an average one-year price target of $287.33, suggesting a 3.74% upside potential. Understanding industry sentiments and analyst opinions is crucial for investors.

data.title: The Fed likely to keep rates unchanged after cutting them several times in 2025. Uncertainty remains on duration of pause.
data.description: Following a string of interest rate cuts in the second half of 2025, the central bank is anticipated to maintain stability by keeping interest rates unchanged on Wednesday. The primary query now revolves around the duration of this anticipated pause in rate adjustments.

data.title: Info-Tech Research Group's report reveals gaps in data quality, governance, and literacy affecting AI adoption in 2026.
data.description: According to the latest Data Priorities 2026 report by Info-Tech Research Group, AI readiness and enterprise decision-making are being hindered by inconsistent data quality, uncertain data governance, and inadequate data literacy. The report indicates that these factors are impeding progress as companies move into 2026. The findings highlight the prevalence of these challenges, emphasizing the need for organizations to address them to improve their data strategies. This underscores the importance of investing in data management processes and training to enhance overall performance.

data.title: Levrx introduces pre-login experience for pharmacy transparency, providing timely info to members.
data.description: In Troy, New York, on January 28, 2026, Levrx emphasized the importance of providing timely information to members about their new pharmacy plan before it goes into effect. Many individuals currently face a lack of access to crucial details about how the plan will affect them. Levrx is dedicated to prioritizing the member experience throughout their healthcare journey, ensuring they are well-informed about upcoming changes.

data.title: Critics question Amazon's $35M 'Melania' film promotion, far exceeding standard documentary budgets.
data.description: The leading technology company is investing a significant $35 million in marketing efforts to support its documentary about the first lady. This amount is considerably higher than the average promotional spending for documentaries.

data.title: Self Protocol launches zero-knowledge proof-of-humanity in Google Web3's Celo Sepolia testnet faucets to filter developers, not bots.
data.description: In San Francisco, Self Protocol has revealed the launch of Self’s zero-knowledge proof-of-humanity on Google Web3’s Celo Sepolia testnet faucets, intending to allocate test tokens to authentic developers rather than automated entities like bots or AI agents. This marks the beginning of a productive partnership between Google Cloud and Self, transitioning the joint effort between the two entities from a mere announcement to a functional stage for Web3 developers. By incorporating Self into the Celo Sepolia faucet process, developers now have the opportunity to seamlessly utilize this feature.

data.title: Fed keeps federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75% after 3 rate cuts in 2025, economic expansion continues at a solid pace.
data.description: The Federal Reserve chose to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75% on Wednesday, pausing its series of rate reductions from last year, which saw three decreases and led to the lowest borrowing costs since 2022. In the January 2026 statement, the Federal Open Market Committee noted that economic activity is growing at a "solid pace," an upgrade from the previous description of "moderate pace." Additionally, they mentioned signs of stability in the unemployment rate, having previously indicated a slight increase. The committee also acknowledged that inflation levels are "somewhat elevated." data.title: Voiset AI Planner's Smart Booking reduces calendar chaos for professionals with native AI scheduling, trusted by 4,000+ teams.
data.description: Using native AI scheduling can prevent double-bookings by cross-referencing actual availability in various workspaces. Essentially, one efficient link streamlines coordination efforts for busy individuals. In an announcement made on Jan. 28, 2026, Voiset, the primary AI voice task organizer relied upon by over 4,000 teams, unveiled this groundbreaking feature.

data.title: Sedgwick introduces Tow and Storage Negotiation Service in its Auto Solutions, aiming to decrease claims costs.

data.title: Daytona Beach gears up for 85th annual Bike Week on Feb. 27-March 8, one of the world's largest motorcycle gatherings.
data.description: In Daytona Beach, Florida, the 85th annual Bike Week will take place from February 27 to March 8, 2026, marking the start of the riding season for motorcycle enthusiasts. This event is among the largest motorcycle gatherings globally and is organized by the Daytona Beach Regional Chamber of Commerce.

data.title: Grant awarded by Unlikely Collaborators to Foundation for Energy Therapies, supporting Integrative Mental Health University.
data.description: In Los Angeles on January 28, 2026, Unlikely Collaborators announced a grant awarded to Foundation for Energy Therapies, Inc. to support their Integrative Mental Health University (IMHU). The grant aims to foster a unique approach that combines scientific research and spiritual teachings. IMHU is dedicated to shaping the future of mental health through this innovative integration.

data.title: Inventor from Oxford, Conn. develops durable snow shovel BKC-712, aiming for more durability compared to standard ones.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 28, 2026, an inventor from Oxford, Connecticut shared his inspiration for creating a new and improved snow shovel. The inventor expressed a desire to address the need for durability in snow shovels, leading to the creation of THE ULTIMATE SNOW SHOVEL. This innovative design offers a sturdier option compared to traditional plastic scoops that often wear out quickly. The product aims to provide a more reliable solution for clearing snow, catering to individuals seeking a more durable and efficient snow removal tool.
